assertions.title: Saved token appears in both from and to autocomplete

steps.action: Navigate to http://localhost:3002/, clear localStorage, switch to Ethereum chain
steps.expected: App loads on Ethereum with clean state
steps.observed: App loaded, Ethereum (1) selected, default tokens USDC/WETH populated

steps.action: Clear From Token field and enter unrecognized address 0x39AA39c021dfbaE8faC545936693aC917d5E7563
steps.expected: Unrecognized Token popup appears with on-chain metadata
steps.observed: Popup appeared showing Name='Compound USD Coin', Symbol='cUSDC', Decimals=8, full address 0x39AA39c021dfbaE8faC545936693aC917d5E7563

steps.action: Click 'Save to Local List' in the popup
steps.expected: Token saved, From Token field shows formatted cUSDC (0x39AA39c021dfbaE8faC545936693aC917d5E7563)
steps.observed: Popup closed, From Token field updated to 'cUSDC (0x39AA39c021dfbaE8faC545936693aC917d5E7563)'

steps.action: Clear To Token field and type 'cUSDC'
steps.expected: cUSDC appears in To Token autocomplete dropdown
steps.observed: Autocomplete dropdown showed cUSDC entry with full address 0x39AA39c021dfbaE8faC545936693aC917d5E7563 and name 'Compound USD Coin'

assertions.title: Multiple lists + local tokens + full disambiguation

steps.action: Open settings panel and add CoinGecko tokenlist URL https://tokens.coingecko.com/uniswap/all.json
steps.expected: CoinGecko list loads with token count
steps.observed: CoinGecko list added with 5098 tokens, shown alongside Default Tokenlist (357 tokens)

steps.action: Close settings and type 'PEPE' in To Token field
steps.expected: Multiple PEPE tokens shown disambiguated by source list name and full address
steps.observed: Autocomplete dropdown showed multiple PEPE entries: (1) PEPE 'Pepe' from 'Default Tokenlist' at 0x6982508145454Ce325dDbE47a25d4ec3d2311933, (2) PEPE 'The Original Pepe' from 'CoinGecko' at 0x4dfae3690b93c47470b03036a17b23c1be05127c, (3) PEPE 'NEWPEPE' from 'CoinGecko' at 0xa57ed6e54be8125bbe45d6ca330e45ebb71ef11e, (4) PEPE 'Pepe King Prawn' from 'CoinGecko' at 0x51a59a02ba906194285e81eb1f98ffa28e7cf4c9. Each entry shows source list badge and full 0x address.

steps.action: Verify local token (cUSDC) coexists with remote list tokens
steps.observed: Searching 'cUSDC' showed CUSDC (Compound USDC) at 0x39aa39c021dfbae8fac545936693ac917d5e7563 — deduplicated by address since same token exists in CoinGecko list (per VAL-AUTO-004)

assertions.title: Toggle local tokens list on/off

steps.action: Open settings panel and inspect Local Tokens section for a toggle switch
steps.expected: Local Tokens section has a toggle switch (like Default Tokenlist and CoinGecko)
steps.observed: Local Tokens section has Export, Import, and individual token Remove buttons, but NO toggle switch. Tokenlist Sources section shows Default Tokenlist toggle and CoinGecko toggle — Local Tokens is not represented as a toggleable source.

steps.action: Programmatically check all toggle/switch elements in the settings panel
steps.expected: At least one toggle in Local Tokens section
steps.observed: Tokenlist Sources section has 2 toggles (Default Tokenlist, CoinGecko). Local Tokens section has 0 toggles.

assertions.issues: The Local Tokens section in the settings panel does not have a toggle switch to enable/disable local tokens as a source. Unlike the Default Tokenlist and custom remote lists which each have a toggle, the Local Tokens section only offers Export, Import, and per-token Remove. There is no way to temporarily hide all local tokens from autocomplete without individually removing them. In the source code (src/server.ts), getTokensForChain() always appends local tokens unconditionally — there is no 'enabled' flag for the local token source.

assertions.title: Chain switch removes wrong-chain local tokens from autocomplete

steps.action: On Ethereum chain, search 'cUSDC' in From Token field
steps.expected: cUSDC local token appears in autocomplete
steps.observed: cUSDC (Compound USD Coin) appeared at 0x39AA39c021dfbaE8faC545936693aC917d5E7563 in autocomplete dropdown
Chain switched to Base (8453), default tokens changed to Base USDC/WETH

steps.action: Search 'cUSDC' in From Token field on Base
steps.expected: No autocomplete results for cUSDC (Ethereum-only local token)
steps.observed: No autocomplete dropdown appeared — cUSDC correctly hidden on Base chain

steps.action: Switch back to Ethereum (1) and search 'cUSDC' again
steps.observed: cUSDC (Compound USD Coin) reappeared at 0x39AA39c021dfbaE8faC545936693aC917d5E7563 in autocomplete dropdown

frictions.description: The From Token input field ID is 'from' (not 'fromToken' as might be expected). Required discovering this via DOM inspection before page.evaluate() calls would work.
frictions.resolution: Queried all input elements to discover correct IDs: 'from', 'to', 'amount', 'slippageBps', 'tokenlistUrlInput'.

frictions.description: Local cUSDC token at 0x39AA39c021dfbaE8faC545936693aC917d5E7563 is deduplicated with CoinGecko's CUSDC (same address). For clean chain-switch testing (VAL-CROSS-005), had to remove CoinGecko list first so cUSDC only existed as a local token.
frictions.resolution: Removed CoinGecko list via settings panel Remove button before testing chain-switch behavior.

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 3 passed, 1 failed. VAL-CROSS-001 confirmed saving unrecognized token via FROM popup makes it available in TO autocomplete. VAL-CROSS-002 confirmed disambiguation with source list badges and full addresses across Default Tokenlist, CoinGecko, and local tokens. VAL-CROSS-003 FAILED — Local Tokens section has no toggle switch; local tokens are always included in autocomplete with no way to temporarily disable them as a source. VAL-CROSS-005 confirmed Ethereum-only local token is hidden on Base and reappears on Ethereum.
